skill.description: Implement NFT standards (ERC-721, ERC-1155) with proper metadata handling, minting strategies, and marketplace integration. Use when creating NFT contracts, building NFT marketplaces, or implementi...
skill.summary: Implement NFT standards (ERC-721, ERC-1155) with proper metadata handling, minting strategies, and m...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 38 static findings are false positives. The skill contains educational Solidity code examples for NFT standards (ERC-721, ERC-1155). The 'require' statements are standard contract validation, 'IERC2981' references are legitimate royalty standards, and 'shell command' flags are misclassified Solidity syntax. This is a legitimate developer tool for NFT smart contract development.

content.value_statement: Build secure NFT collections using ERC-721 and ERC-1155 standards with proper metadata, royalties, and marketplace integration.
actual_capabilities: Implement ERC-1155 multi-token standard for gaming items
Create metadata standards with IPFS or on-chain storage
Add royalty support using EIP-2981 standard
Build soulbound tokens that cannot be transferred
Implement dynamic NFTs that evolve over time
limitations: Does not deploy contracts to blockchain
Does not interact with live NFT marketplaces

use_cases.title: Create NFT Art Collection
use_cases.description: Build a gas-optimized NFT collection with reveal mechanism and royalty distribution

use_cases.title: Build Gaming Item System
use_cases.description: Implement multi-token standard for game assets with varying supply and attributes

use_cases.title: Create Credential System
use_cases.description: Use soulbound tokens for non-transferable credentials and achievements

prompt_templates.title: Basic NFT Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ERC-721 NFT contract with OpenZeppelin. Include minting with payment, URI storage, and withdrawal function.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new NFT collection project

prompt_templates.title: Gaming Items Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement an ERC-1155 contract for game items with max supply limits, batch minting, and burn functionality.

prompt_templates.title: Royalty-Enabled Marketplace NFT
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an NFT contract with EIP-2981 royalties, configurable fee recipient, and marketplace compatibility.

prompt_templates.title: Dynamic Evolving NFT
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a dynamic NFT that changes based on external state. Include experience points and level-up mechanics.

output_examples.input: Create an ERC-721 contract with minting
output: Complete Solidity contract using OpenZeppelin ERC721URIStorage
Minting function with quantity limits and payment validation

output_examples.input: Add royalties to my NFT collection
output: Marketplace integration notes for OpenSea, LooksRare

best_practices: Use OpenZeppelin contracts for battle-tested security
Pin IPFS metadata with a pinning service for reliability
Implement EIP-2981 royalties for marketplace compatibility
Use ERC721A for gas-optimized batch minting
anti_patterns: Storing images directly on-chain (use IPFS or external hosting)
Skipping access controls on mint and burn functions
Not implementing supportsInterface for full compatibility

faq.question: What is the difference between ERC-721 and ERC-1155?
faq.answer: ERC-721 is for unique non-fungible tokens where each token is one-of-a-kind. ERC-1155 is a multi-token standard that supports both fungible (currency) and non-fungible tokens in a single contract, ideal for gaming.

faq.question: How do I add royalties to my NFT collection?
faq.answer: Implement the EIP-2981 royalty standard. This allows marketplaces like OpenSea and LooksRare to automatically calculate and distribute royalties on secondary sales.

faq.question: What is IPFS and why do NFTs use it?
faq.answer: IPFS is a distributed file storage system. NFTs store metadata (images, attributes) on IPFS because it provides permanent, decentralized storage without a single point of failure.

faq.question: Can NFTs be made non-transferable?
faq.answer: Yes, soulbound tokens (SBTs) use the _beforeTokenTransfer override to block transfers after minting. This is useful for credentials and achievements.

faq.question: What is ERC721A and why is it popular?
faq.answer: ERC721A is a gas-optimized implementation of ERC-721 that reduces minting costs by up to 90% when minting multiple tokens in a single transaction.

faq.question: Do I need to test NFT contracts before deployment?
faq.answer: Yes, always test thoroughly. Use tools like Hardhat or Foundry to write comprehensive test suites covering minting, transfers, approvals, and edge cases.
